searchDocs.title: seed​
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> Perlin.seed: number The seed this sampler was constructed with.

searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> Perlin.new( seed: number?-- Any number. Defaults to a random seed. ) → Perlin Creates a new Perlin sampler. Equal seeds always produce identical noise.

searchDocs.title: sample2D​
searchDocs.content: </> Perlin:sample2D( x: number, y: number ) → number-- Noise value in [-1, 1]. Samples 2D Perlin noise.

searchDocs.title: sample2DWithDerivative​
searchDocs.content: </> Perlin:sample2DWithDerivative( x: number, y: number ) → ( number,-- Noise value in [-1, 1]. number,-- d/dx. number-- d/dy. ) Samples 2D Perlin noise along with its analytical derivative — the exact slope of the noise field, with no extra noise samples.

searchDocs.title: sample3D​
searchDocs.content: </> Perlin:sample3D( x: number, y: number, z: number ) → number-- Noise value in [-1, 1]. Samples 3D Perlin noise.

searchDocs.title: sample3DWithDerivative​
searchDocs.content: </> Perlin:sample3DWithDerivative( x: number, y: number, z: number ) → ( number,-- Noise value in [-1, 1]. number,-- d/dx. number,-- d/dy. number-- d/dz. ) Samples 3D Perlin noise along with its analytical derivative.

searchDocs.title: Atlas
searchDocs.content: Atlas A procedural generation toolkit for Roblox: seeded noise with analytical derivatives (Noise), point scattering (Sampling), response curves (Curve), and on-screen debugging (Visualizer). local Atlas = require(path.to.Atlas) local simplex = Atlas.Noise.Simplex.new(42) local falloff = Atlas.Curve.new({ Vector2.new(0, 0), Vector2.new(0.6, 0.15), Vector2.new(1, 1), }) local height = falloff:evaluate(Atlas.Noise.fbm2D(simplex, x * 0.01, z * 0.01, 4) * 0.5 + 0.5)

searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> Curve.new( points: {Vector2 }-- At least two control points. ) → Curve Creates a curve through the given control points. Points are sorted by x on construction; x values must be distinct.

searchDocs.title: evaluate​
searchDocs.content: </> Curve:evaluate(x: number) → number Evaluates the curve at x. Inputs outside the control point domain clamp to the first or last point's value.

searchDocs.title: map​
searchDocs.content: </> Curve:map(values: {number}) → {number} Evaluates the curve over a whole array in one call.

searchDocs.title: domain​
searchDocs.content: </> Curve:domain() → ( number,-- Smallest control point x. number-- Largest control point x. ) Returns the x extent covered by the control points.

searchDocs.title: range​
searchDocs.content: </> Curve:range() → ( number,-- Smallest output value. number-- Largest output value. ) Returns the output extent of the curve. Because the spline is monotone between control points, this is exactly the min and max control point y.

searchDocs.title: uniform​
searchDocs.content: </> Sampling.uniform( width: number, height: number, count: number, seed: number?-- Any number. Defaults to a random seed. ) → {Vector3 } Scatters count independent uniformly-distributed points.

searchDocs.title: poissonDisc​
searchDocs.content: </> Sampling.poissonDisc( width: number, height: number, radius: number,-- Minimum distance between any two points. seed: number?,-- Any number. Defaults to a random seed. maxAttempts: number?-- Candidates tried per active point. Defaults to 30. ) → {Vector3 } Scatters points with blue-noise spacing using Bridson's Poisson disc algorithm: no two points are closer than radius, and the region is filled until no more fit. Runs in O(n) via a background acceleration grid.

searchDocs.title: blueNoiseGrid​
searchDocs.content: </> Sampling.blueNoiseGrid( width: number,-- Width of the region in studs/units. height: number,-- Height of the region in studs/units. cellSize: number,-- Size of each grid cell (controls spacing). seed: number?,-- Optional seed for deterministic generation. density: number?-- Optional [0..1] density multiplier (default 1). ) → {Vector3 }-- Array of generated points. (x, y, 0) Generates a blue-noise-like point distribution using a jittered grid. Outputs a vector3 because they are much faster internally.

searchDocs.title: NoiseSampler2D​
searchDocs.content: </> interface NoiseSampler2D { sample2D: ( self: any, x: number, y: number ) → number } Anything with a sample2D method — every Atlas noise sampler qualifies, as do user-defined wrappers.

searchDocs.title: NoiseSampler3D​
searchDocs.content: </> interface NoiseSampler3D { sample3D: ( self: any, x: number, y: number, z: number ) → number } Anything with a sample3D method.

searchDocs.title: fbm2D​
searchDocs.content: </> Noise.fbm2D( sampler: NoiseSampler2D, x: number, y: number, octaves: number, lacunarity: number?,-- Frequency multiplier per octave. Defaults to 2. gain: number?-- Amplitude multiplier per octave. Defaults to 0.5. ) → number Layers 2D noise octaves into fractional Brownian motion. The result is renormalized by total amplitude, so a sampler emitting [-1, 1] stays in [-1, 1] regardless of octave count.

searchDocs.title: fbm3D​
searchDocs.content: </> Noise.fbm3D( sampler: NoiseSampler3D, x: number, y: number, z: number, octaves: number, lacunarity: number?,-- Frequency multiplier per octave. Defaults to 2. gain: number?-- Amplitude multiplier per octave. Defaults to 0.5. ) → number Layers 3D noise octaves into fractional Brownian motion.

searchDocs.title: Presets​
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> Visualizer.Presets: Presets Built-in pixel and draw callbacks for noise, curves, and point sets.

searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> Visualizer.new(options: VisualizerOptions?) → Visualizer

interface VisualizerOptions { width: number?-- Image width in pixels, at most 1024. Defaults to 256. height: number?-- Image height in pixels, at most 1024. Defaults to 256. parent: Instance? -- Where to parent the window. Defaults to the local player's PlayerGui. title: string?-- Window title. Defaults to "Atlas". pixelScale: number?-- On-screen magnification of the image. Defaults to 1. }  Creates a visualizer window. On the server (or in other headless contexts)options.parent must be provided, since there is no local PlayerGui.

searchDocs.title: render​
searchDocs.content: </> Visualizer:render(callback: PixelCallback) → () Renders the image by calling callback once per pixel. Returned channels are clamped to [0, 1].

searchDocs.title: draw​
searchDocs.content: </> Visualizer:draw(callback: DrawCallback) → () Hands the raw RGBA buffer to callback for direct writes, then uploads it. Faster than Visualizer:render when the callback can batch its own work, and the natural fit for Presets.curve and Presets.points.

searchDocs.title: destroy​
searchDocs.content: </> Visualizer:destroy() → () Closes the window and releases the EditableImage. Safe to call twice.

searchDocs.title: noise​
searchDocs.content: </> Presets.noise( sampler: NoiseSampler2D, options: NoisePresetOptions? ) → PixelCallback

interface NoisePresetOptions { scale: number?-- Noise-space units per pixel. Defaults to 0.05. offsetX: number?-- Noise-space x offset. Defaults to 0. offsetY: number?-- Noise-space y offset. Defaults to 0. rangeMin: number?-- Sampler output mapped to black. Defaults to -1. rangeMax: number?-- Sampler output mapped to white. Defaults to 1. curve: Curve?-- Optional response curve applied after range mapping. }  Builds a grayscale [PixelCallback] for any 2D noise sampler. Sampler output is mapped from [rangeMin, rangeMax] to black–white, optionally shaped through a Curve — handy for previewing exactly the remap your terrain will use. Pass rangeMin = 0 for Voronoi distances.

searchDocs.title: curve​
searchDocs.content: </> Presets.curve(curve: Curve) → DrawCallback Builds a [DrawCallback] that plots a Curve over a quarter grid, scaled to the curve's own domain and range.

searchDocs.title: points​
searchDocs.content: </> Presets.points( points: {Vector2 }, options: PointsPresetOptions ) → DrawCallback

interface PointsPresetOptions { width: number-- Width of the region the points were sampled from. height: number-- Height of the region the points were sampled from. radius: number?-- Dot radius in pixels. Defaults to 2. }  Builds a [DrawCallback] that plots sample points as dots, mapping the sampled region onto the full image.

searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> Simplex.seed: number The seed this sampler was constructed with.

searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> Simplex.new( seed: number?-- Any number. Defaults to a random seed. ) → Simplex Creates a new simplex sampler. Equal seeds always produce identical noise.

searchDocs.title: sample2D​
searchDocs.content: </> Simplex:sample2D( x: number, y: number ) → number-- Noise value in approximately [-1, 1]. Samples 2D simplex noise.

searchDocs.title: sample2DWithDerivative​
searchDocs.content: </> Simplex:sample2DWithDerivative( x: number, y: number ) → ( number,-- Noise value in approximately [-1, 1]. number,-- d/dx. number-- d/dy. ) Samples 2D simplex noise along with its analytical derivative.

searchDocs.title: sample3D​
searchDocs.content: </> Simplex:sample3D( x: number, y: number, z: number ) → number-- Noise value in approximately [-1, 1]. Samples 3D simplex noise.

searchDocs.title: sample3DWithDerivative​
searchDocs.content: </> Simplex:sample3DWithDerivative( x: number, y: number, z: number ) → ( number,-- Noise value in approximately [-1, 1]. number,-- d/dx. number,-- d/dy. number-- d/dz. ) Samples 3D simplex noise along with its analytical derivative.

searchDocs.title: seed​
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> Voronoi.seed: number The seed this sampler was constructed with.

searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> Voronoi.new( seed: number?-- Any number. Defaults to a random seed. ) → Voronoi Creates a new Voronoi sampler. Equal seeds always produce identical noise.

searchDocs.title: sample2D​
searchDocs.content: </> Voronoi:sample2D( x: number, y: number ) → number-- F1, the distance to the nearest feature point. Samples 2D Voronoi noise.

searchDocs.title: sample3D​
searchDocs.content: </> Voronoi:sample3D( x: number, y: number, z: number ) → number-- F1, the distance to the nearest feature point. Samples 3D Voronoi noise.

searchDocs.title: cell2D​
searchDocs.content: </> Voronoi:cell2D( x: number, y: number ) → ( number,-- F1, the distance to the nearest feature point. number,-- F2, the distance to the second-nearest feature point. number-- Integer id of the nearest cell. ) Samples 2D Voronoi noise, returning the two nearest feature distances and a stable id for the winning cell. f2 - f1 produces ridged cell borders; the id is useful for seeding per-cell content such as biomes.

searchDocs.title: cell3D​
searchDocs.content: </> Voronoi:cell3D( x: number, y: number, z: number ) → ( number,-- F1, the distance to the nearest feature point. number,-- F2, the distance to the second-nearest feature point. number-- Integer id of the nearest cell. ) Samples 3D Voronoi noise, returning the two nearest feature distances and a stable id for the winning cell.

searchDocs.title: sample2DWithDerivative​
searchDocs.content: </> Voronoi:sample2DWithDerivative( x: number, y: number, epsilon: number?-- Finite-difference step. Defaults to 1e-3. ) → ( number,-- F1, the distance to the nearest feature point. number,-- d/dx. number-- d/dy. ) Samples 2D Voronoi noise along with its slope, estimated by central finite differences. Worley noise has no clean analytical gradient, and the distance field is not differentiable on cell borders.

searchDocs.title: sample3DWithDerivative​
searchDocs.content: </> Voronoi:sample3DWithDerivative( x: number, y: number, z: number, epsilon: number?-- Finite-difference step. Defaults to 1e-3. ) → ( number,-- F1, the distance to the nearest feature point. number,-- d/dx. number,-- d/dy. number-- d/dz. ) Samples 3D Voronoi noise along with its slope, estimated by central finite differences.
